sections.title: Is New York City Safe for Tourists in 2026?
sections.summary: Short answer: yes. NYC is generally safe for tourists in 2026, particularly around Midtown, Times Square, and the main attractions. For most visitors the real risk is petty theft like pickpocketing, not violent crime.
bullets: New York is one of the most visited cities in the world, and it's walkable and busy at almost any hour. For solo travelers, women included, that mix of independence and constant activity is a big part of the appeal — but like any big city it pays to stay aware and make sensible calls.
This guide skips the vague advice and the scare stories. It works through actual situations instead — the subway at night, street interactions — with a clear plan for each.
Whether you're in a Manhattan museum, walking around Brooklyn, or catching a late train, it comes down to the same thing: stay aware, and know what to do if something feels off.
